summary: A NATO member and G20 economy straddling Europe and Asia, leveraging its unique geography to play all sides — mediating between Russia and Ukraine while buying Russian gas and selling drones to Ukraine.

history.detail: Osman I founds empire that will dominate Middle East, North Africa, Eastern Europe for 600 years.
Ottomans conquer Byzantine capital; renamed Istanbul.
Ottoman westward expansion halted; slow decline begins.
Atatürk's revolution; secular modernisation, alphabet reform.
Key NATO member controlling Bosphorus Strait.
Occupies northern Cyprus; still unresolved dispute.
Islamist-rooted party begins reshaping secular Turkey.
Erdoğan blames Gülen movement; massive purge of military, judiciary, media.

finance.currency_role: Lira (TRY); suffered extreme inflation/devaluation under Erdoğan's unorthodox monetary policy
finance.central_bank: Central Bank of the Republic of Turkey (1930)

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Growing; Trendyol is Turkey's first unicorn ($16B)

resources.oil_gas: None domestic; major transit hub for Caspian gas to Europe
resources.rare_earths: Significant boron reserves (70% of world supply)
resources.water: Water stress; GAP dam project on Euphrates tensions with Syria/Iraq
resources.agriculture: Major hazelnut, cotton, wheat, tomato exporter

follow_the_money.detail: Montreux Convention gives Turkey control over strait; key leverage vs Russia
Turkey hosts 3.6M Syrians; €6B EU deal for containment
Bayraktar TB2 exported to 25+ countries; reshaping warfare economics

theories_disputed.detail: 32 countries officially recognise as genocide; Turkey officially denies using the term.
Rapid response and beneficiary position led to widespread suspicion of staged or allowed coup.

connections.detail: Buys Russian gas; Akkuyu nuclear plant; TurkStream pipeline
Sells Bayraktar drones; mediates Black Sea grain deal
NATO ally; hosts Incirlik Air Base (nuclear weapons)
Ongoing disputes over Aegean, Cyprus, gas exploration
